hero.headline: UPES Online MBA in Infrastructure Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: University of Petroleum and Energy Studies (UPES) Online MBA with an Infrastructure Management specialisation. NIRF #45 University, NIRF #36 Management, NAAC A, UGC-DEB approved. Infrastructure Project Management, Urban Infrastructure Planning, PPP Models and Financing, Infrastructure Regulation, Construction Management, Smart Cities, Environmental Management, and Infrastructure Asset Management in Sem 3-4.

sections.tldr: UPES Online MBA Infrastructure Management: NIRF #45 University, NIRF #36 Management, NAAC A, fees Rs 1,75,000 (Year 1: Rs 95,000 / Year 2: Rs 80,000), zero-cost EMI at Rs 7,292/month. Sector-specific curriculum covering Infrastructure Project Management, PPP Financing, Urban Planning, Construction Management, Smart Cities, Environmental Management, and Infrastructure Asset Management.

about.body:
The UPES Online MBA in Infrastructure Management is offered by University of Petroleum and Energy Studies, Dehradun. UPES holds NAAC A accreditation, NIRF #45 University ranking (2025), and NIRF #36 in Management (2025). The online programme is UGC-DEB approved.

The Infrastructure Management specialisation delivers Sem 3-4 subjects covering the full infrastructure project lifecycle from planning to asset management. Sem 3 covers Infrastructure Project Management, Urban and Rural Infrastructure Planning, Public-Private Partnership Models and Financing, Infrastructure Regulation and Governance, and Construction Management. Sem 4 covers Strategic Management, Smart Cities and Digital Infrastructure, Environmental and Social Impact Management, Infrastructure Asset Management, and Dissertation.

India's National Infrastructure Pipeline (NIP) commits Rs 111 lakh crore to infrastructure investment through 2025, creating persistent demand for professionals who combine infrastructure domain knowledge with business management credentials. UPES's engineering and energy sector heritage positions this specialisation specifically for professionals in construction, roads, ports, airports, urban infrastructure, and power infrastructure who need credentialled management skills to move into project leadership, PPP advisory, and asset management roles.

sem3Subjects.name: Infrastructure Project Management
sem3Subjects.description: Apply infrastructure project lifecycle management, manage contract structures (EPC, BOT, HAM), and implement earned value analysis for large capital projects.

sem3Subjects.name: Urban and Rural Infrastructure Planning
sem3Subjects.description: Analyse urban master planning frameworks, manage rural infrastructure programme delivery, and evaluate infrastructure gap assessment methodologies.

sem3Subjects.name: Public-Private Partnership Models and Financing
sem3Subjects.description: Evaluate BOT, BOOT, DBFOT, and hybrid annuity PPP models, structure project financing, and analyse concession agreement frameworks for infrastructure roles.

sem3Subjects.name: Infrastructure Regulation and Governance
sem3Subjects.description: Navigate regulatory frameworks for roads, ports, airports, and energy infrastructure, and manage compliance with sector regulators and government agencies.

sem3Subjects.name: Construction Management
sem3Subjects.description: Apply construction project scheduling, resource allocation, quality management, and safety management systems for infrastructure project execution roles.

sem4Subjects.name: Strategic Management
sem4Subjects.description: Develop infrastructure company competitive strategy, evaluate portfolio management of infrastructure assets, and design business expansion in infrastructure sectors.

sem4Subjects.name: Smart Cities and Digital Infrastructure
sem4Subjects.description: Evaluate smart city technology deployment, manage digital infrastructure projects, and apply data-driven urban management frameworks for smart city roles.

sem4Subjects.name: Environmental and Social Impact Management
sem4Subjects.description: Manage environmental impact assessments, apply social impact frameworks, and design stakeholder engagement programmes for infrastructure project compliance.

sem4Subjects.name: Infrastructure Asset Management
sem4Subjects.description: Apply lifecycle asset management frameworks, manage infrastructure O&M contracts, and design asset performance monitoring systems for infrastructure owners.

sem4Subjects.name: Dissertation
sem4Subjects.description: Complete an applied infrastructure management research project on PPP structure optimisation, smart city implementation, or infrastructure asset lifecycle management.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 shared core subjects across all UPES MBA specialisations. Core includes Managerial Economics, Accounting for Managers, Marketing Management, Quantitative Techniques, Operations Management, OB and HRM, Project Management, Financial Management, Research Methodology, and Business Analytics for Managers.

whoHires.body:
Infrastructure Management MBA graduates from UPES Online are hired across these industries and roles:

**Industries:** Roads and highways, Airports, Ports and terminals, Urban infrastructure and smart cities, Power infrastructure, Real estate development, Infrastructure consulting

**Entry-level roles:** Infrastructure Project Analyst, Business Development Executive (Infrastructure), PPP Advisory Analyst, Construction Manager Trainee, Asset Management Analyst

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Infrastructure Project Manager, PPP Advisory Manager, Business Development Manager, Asset Operations Manager, Smart Cities Project Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** Infrastructure Investment Director, Head of Projects, VP Business Development, CEO (Infrastructure Company), Head of Asset Management

**Top hiring organisations (UPES alumni placements):** L&T Infrastructure, GMR Group, GVK Group, Adani Ports, IRB Infrastructure, NHAI project offices, NITI Aayog PPP cell, Deloitte Infrastructure Advisory, KPMG Infrastructure, and state PWD and urban development authority project management units.

skills.body:
The UPES Infrastructure Management specialisation builds skills that construction, PPP advisory, and infrastructure owner companies test at project manager and business development manager levels:

**Technical skills:**
- Infrastructure Project Management: EPC, BOT, and HAM contract structure management, earned value analysis, and infrastructure project lifecycle management for project leadership roles
- Public-Private Partnership Models and Financing: BOT, BOOT, DBFOT, and HAM model evaluation, concession agreement analysis, and project financing structure for PPP advisory and infrastructure finance roles
- Infrastructure Asset Management: lifecycle asset management framework design, O&M contract management, and asset performance monitoring for infrastructure owner and operator roles
- Construction Management: project scheduling, resource allocation, quality management, and site safety systems for infrastructure project execution roles

**Applied skills:**
- Smart Cities and Digital Infrastructure: smart city technology evaluation and digital infrastructure project management for urban development and smart city programme roles
- Environmental and Social Impact Management: EIA framework management, social impact assessment, and stakeholder engagement for infrastructure project compliance and CSR roles

**Soft skills:** Presenting infrastructure investment proposals to government stakeholders, negotiating concession agreement terms with private sector partners, managing multi-disciplinary project teams at infrastructure sites, and communicating infrastructure project performance to boards and lenders.

comparisons.body:
Comparing UPES Online MBA Infrastructure Management against peers:

**vs Amity Online MBA Infrastructure Management:** Amity is NAAC A+ with strong all-India brand recognition at Rs 2,25,000. UPES Infrastructure Management at Rs 1,75,000 includes Smart Cities and Digital Infrastructure as a dedicated Sem 4 subject alongside PPP Models and Infrastructure Asset Management. UPES's NIRF #36 Management ranking is stronger than Amity's management category performance. UPES's energy and infrastructure sector alumni network is more concentrated in infrastructure sector roles.

**vs general MBA with project management focus:** General MBA programmes with project management electives do not deliver dedicated Infrastructure Regulation, PPP Models, or Infrastructure Asset Management subjects. UPES Infrastructure Management at Rs 1,75,000 provides sector-specific curriculum at fees comparable to peer general online MBA programmes.

**vs UPES Oil and Gas or Power Management specialisations:** UPES sector-specific MBAs in Oil and Gas (Rs 2,20,000) and Power Management (Rs 2,20,000) are priced at a premium tier. Infrastructure Management at Rs 1,75,000 covers energy, transport, and urban infrastructure broadly. For professionals at cross-sector infrastructure companies (L&T, Adani, GMR spanning roads, ports, and energy), Infrastructure Management covers a wider asset class range.

fees.body:
The UPES Online MBA in Infrastructure Management has a total program fee of Rs 1,75,000 across 2 years (4 semesters).

**Year-wise fee schedule:**
- Year 1 (Semesters 1 and 2): Rs 95,000
- Year 2 (Semesters 3 and 4): Rs 80,000

**Zero-cost EMI (no-cost EMI available):**
- Rs 7,292 per month for 24 months
- Tenure options: 3 to 36 months
- Zero interest (0% cost EMI) through UPES NBFC lending tie-ups
- Contact our counsellor for current tenure options and approval terms

**Scholarships available (confirmed from UPES scholarships portal):**
- **Academic/Work Experience scholarship**: 10% for 3+ years experience with 55%+ graduation marks; 15% for 5+ years experience; 20% for 10+ years experience
- **Alumni scholarship**: 20% for UPES degree programme graduates
- **Vijay (Sports) scholarship**: 35% to 100% based on national/international achievement level
- Scholarships cannot be combined with corporate MOU discounts
- Scholarship benefit applied semester-wise; eligibility confirmed at admission

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with UPES each quarter. Please reconfirm current fees, scholarship eligibility, and EMI terms with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body: The degree certificate is issued by University of Petroleum and Energy Studies (UPES) and carries the UPES name with the MBA designation. The Infrastructure Management specialisation is noted on the marksheet and transcript. Online mode is identified per UGC-DEB 2020 regulations. The certificate is valid for corporate hiring, PSU eligibility, and higher education admission across India. UPES is WES recognised, making the degree suitable for international credential evaluation.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from UPES Online MBA Infrastructure Management alumni. Ratings from post-completion surveys.

items.name: Anil Sharma
items.liked: PPP Models and Financing was outstanding and directly applicable to my role in NHAI project advisory. Infrastructure Asset Management gave frameworks for O&M contract review work. UPES alumni at L&T and GMR were directly helpful.
items.disliked: Smart Cities subject could benefit from deeper case studies on Pune and Bhopal smart city implementations rather than generic smart city frameworks.
items.body: I had 6 years in roads infrastructure and the UPES Infrastructure MBA gave me the business credentials for a project management leadership role. The PPP subject alone was worth the programme fee.

items.name: Reena Kapoor
items.liked: Environmental and Social Impact Management and Construction Management were well-structured. Infrastructure Regulation subject covered NHAI and AAI frameworks in useful depth.
items.disliked: For South India infrastructure careers, UPES brand recognition is less strong than in North India. Self-sourcing was needed for Bengaluru or Hyderabad infrastructure roles.
items.body: Good programme for North India infrastructure sector careers. UPES brand is well known at construction and infrastructure conglomerates active in North India. NIRF #36 Management ranking is a genuine credential differentiator.

items.name: Rajendra Bisht
items.liked: Infrastructure Project Management subject was well-structured with EPC and HAM contract coverage. Urban Planning gave useful frameworks for smart city work.
items.disliked: More industry practitioner interactions from NHAI, NMIA, and port authority managers would strengthen the programme.
items.body: Strong infrastructure credential at Rs 1,75,000 with zero-cost EMI. Best for North India roads, power infrastructure, and urban development careers. UPES alumni network in infrastructure sector is genuinely differentiated.

faqs.question: What is the total fee for UPES Online MBA Infrastructure Management?
faqs.answer: The total program fee is Rs 1,75,000 across 2 years. Year 1 is Rs 95,000 and Year 2 is Rs 80,000. Zero-cost EMI is available at Rs 7,292 per month for 24 months. Reconfirm current fees with our counsellor before payment.

faqs.question: What is PPP in UPES Infrastructure Management MBA?
faqs.answer: Public-Private Partnership Models and Financing is a dedicated Sem 3 subject covering BOT, BOOT, DBFOT, and Hybrid Annuity Model PPP structures, project financing frameworks, and concession agreement analysis. It is directly applicable to professionals in NHAI, AAI, port, and urban infrastructure PPP projects.

faqs.question: Is UPES Infrastructure Management suitable for construction and real estate professionals?
faqs.answer: Yes. Construction Management in Sem 3 covers EPC project scheduling, resource allocation, quality management, and site safety systems. Infrastructure Asset Management in Sem 4 covers O&M lifecycle frameworks applicable to real estate and infrastructure asset owners. The specialisation is relevant for construction, infrastructure, and real estate professionals targeting management roles.

faqs.question: What scholarships does UPES offer for Online MBA?
faqs.answer: UPES offers Academic/Work Experience scholarship (10% for 3+ years, 15% for 5+ years, 20% for 10+ years experience with 55%+ graduation), Alumni scholarship (20%), and Vijay Sports scholarship (35% to 100%). Eligibility confirmed at admission.

faqs.question: How does UPES Infrastructure Management compare to Oil and Gas or Power Management specialisations?
faqs.answer: UPES Infrastructure Management at Rs 1,75,000 covers cross-sector infrastructure spanning roads, ports, airports, urban, and energy infrastructure broadly. Oil and Gas Management and Power Management at Rs 2,20,000 deliver deeper sector-specific curriculum for petroleum and electricity sector professionals respectively. Infrastructure Management is the right choice for professionals at multi-sector infrastructure conglomerates or those targeting PPP advisory careers.

chooseIf: You want a NAAC A, NIRF #36 Management infrastructure MBA with PPP, project management, and smart city curriculum at Rs 1,75,000.
You are targeting infrastructure project manager, PPP advisory, or asset management roles at roads, ports, airports, or urban infrastructure companies.
You want zero-cost EMI at Rs 7,292/month and WES-recognised degree credentials.
consider: If oil and gas sector is the career focus, UPES Online MBA Oil and Gas Management delivers petroleum sector-specific curriculum at Rs 2,20,000.
If power sector careers are the goal, UPES Online MBA Power Management covers electricity market, renewable project, and smart grid subjects at Rs 2,20,000.

If operations management with manufacturing and supply chain focus is more relevant than infrastructure, UPES Online MBA Operations Management at Rs 1,75,000 covers a broader operations management curriculum.
